subtitle: Sungam Tavirtta Cholan – The Emperor Who Loosened the Yoke

panels.shot: low-angle wide of a regal king on a stone mandapa, trade caravans and ships faint in the background
panels.action: Kulottunga Chola I stands tall on a temple mandapa, one hand resting on his sword hilt, the other raised slightly as if blessing distant caravans and ships that stretch from Kaveri riverbanks to the eastern sea horizon.

dialogue.text: In the later glory of the Cholas rose Kulottunga, lord of Kaveri and Godavari, famed not for conquest alone, but as Sungam Tavirtta Cholan, the king who abolished tolls.

i18n.hi: चोलों की उत्तर की महिमा में कुलोतुंग उठा, कावेरी–गोदावरी का स्वामी, सिर्फ़ विजय से नहीं, बल्कि ‘सुनगम तविरत्ता चोलन’, चुंगी मिटाने वाला राजा कहलाया।

panels.shot: sweeping bird's-eye of the Tamil country from delta to coast
panels.action: A vast bird's-eye view shows the fertile Kaveri delta, temple towns with tall gopurams, winding rivers, bustling ports, and roads lined with bullock carts and traders moving between village and sea.

dialogue.text: By the eleventh century, the Chola realm stretched from the Kaveri to the Godavari, its temples rich, its ports crowded, its kings heirs to a century of conquest and sea-borne power.

i18n.hi: ग्यारहवीं सदी तक चोल राज्य कावेरी से गोदावरी तक फैला था — मंदिर समृद्ध, बंदरगाह ठसाठस, और राजा सौ साल की जीतों व समुंदरी शक्ति के वारिस।

panels.shot: medium shot of a young prince in an Eastern Chalukya court
panels.action: A younger Kulottunga Chola I, still prince, stands in a richly carved wooden hall of the Eastern Chalukya country, wearing mixed Deccan and Tamil ornaments, listening intently as older nobles argue before a throne draped in Eastern regalia.

dialogue.text: Kulottunga was born of two lineages: Chola on one side, Eastern Chalukya on the other, a bridge between Kaveri and Godavari, trained amid rival courts and watchful feudatories.

i18n.hi: कुलोतुंग दो वंशों से जन्मा — एक ओर चोल, दूसरी ओर पूर्वी चालुक्य, कावेरी–गोदावरी के बीच का सेतु, जो प्रतिद्वंद्वी दरबारों और चौकस सामंतों के बीच पला।

panels.shot: slow push-in on his face as he studies a map
panels.action: Kulottunga leans over a palm-leaf map spread on a low table, lamps casting warm light on his focused eyes as he traces routes from inland granaries to coastal ports with a slender finger.

dialogue.text: In a world of shifting vassals and ambitious neighbors, he learned that roads and rivers, merchants and granaries, mattered as much as spears and elephants.

i18n.hi: बदलते जागीरदारों और野सियों भरे इस दौर में उसने सीखा कि सड़कें–नदियाँ, व्यापारी–अनाज-कोठार, भालों और हाथियों जितने ही ज़रूरी हैं।

panels.shot: dramatic interior wide of the imperial audience hall at Gangaikonda Cholapuram
panels.action: The great stone-pillared hall of Gangaikonda Cholapuram looms, crowds of courtiers lined on either side as Kulottunga Chola I, now older and crowned, ascends the high throne with a composed but wary expression.

dialogue.text: Around 1070, in the long sunset of Chola might, Kulottunga took the lion throne, inheriting not only crown and titles, but also unrest, drained treasuries, and the weight of past wars.

i18n.hi: लगभग 1070 में, चोल शक्ति के ढलते सूरज में, कुलोतुंग ने सिंहासन संभाला — साथ मिला मुकुट ही नहीं, अव्यवस्था, खाली ख़ज़ाने और पुराने युद्धों का बोझ भी।

panels.shot: wide shot of temple courtyard with scribes and stone masons at work on inscriptions
panels.action: In a temple courtyard, stone masons carve neat Grantha and Tamil letters into a slab while scribes nearby unroll palm-leaf records as Kulottunga stands in the shade, watching with a serious, reflective gaze.

dialogue.text: Inscriptions would become his voice, chiselled into stone. They speak of a ruler who knew the pen and the levy could wound as surely as the sword.

i18n.hi: शिलालेख उसकी ज़ुबान बन गए, पत्थर पर खुदे हुए। वे बताते हैं एक ऐसे शासक की कथा जिसे पता था कि कलम और कर, तलवार जितने ही घाव दे सकते हैं।

panels.shot: busy road-level shot among traders at a toll gate
panels.action: On a dusty road near a river crossing, traders with laden bullock carts are halted before a wooden toll barrier, a tax official counting bundles as poor peasants wait in a weary line; Kulottunga watches from horseback in the background, cloaked and observing.

dialogue.text: Across his lands, toll posts pricked the roads like thorns. Each river ferry, each market gate, demanded its due from carts, cattle, cloth, and grain.

i18n.hi: उसके राज्य भर में चुंगी चौकियाँ सड़कों को काँटों की तरह चुभती थीं। हर नाव घाट, हर किले-द्वार, गाड़ियों, मवेशियों, कपड़े और अनाज से अपना हिस्सा वसूलता।

panels.shot: medium close-up of Kulottunga observing a distressed family at the toll
panels.action: Kulottunga’s face softens as he watches a poor woman clutching a small child, anxiously counting a few coins while a toll guard shakes his head, pointing to the barrier and the piled goods beyond.

dialogue.text: For rich guilds and caravans, tolls were nuisance and cost. For the humble cart of a peasant or a widow, they were a wall across the road to survival.

i18n.hi: धनी संघों और काफ़िलों के लिए चुंगी झंझट और ख़र्च थी; किसान या विधवा की छोटी गाड़ी के लिए, ये तो जैसे जीने की राह पर दीवार थी।

panels.shot: interior medium shot of council with Brahmanas and ministers seated
panels.action: Inside a lamplit council chamber, Brahmana advisors and seasoned ministers sit cross-legged in debate, some frowning, some worried, as Kulottunga sits at the head, hands clasped, listening with a calm but firm gaze.

dialogue.speaker: kulottunga_chola_i
dialogue.text: If the road is choked with tolls, how will our people breathe?

i18n.hi: अगर सड़कें चुंगी से घुट जाएँ, तो हमारे लोग साँस कैसे लें?

panels.shot: close-up on a palm-leaf order being written and sealed
panels.action: A royal scribe carefully inscribes a palm leaf with a stylus, recording an order, while a royal signet ring bearing the tiger emblem presses into a clay seal beside it; Kulottunga’s hand hovers approvingly nearby.

dialogue.text: After counsel and calculation, the order went forth: many road tolls and internal transit duties were to be swept away, their knots loosed from the necks of traders and farmers.

i18n.hi: लंबी सलाह–मशविरा और हिसाब–किताब के बाद हुक्म निकला: कई सड़क चुंगियाँ और अंदरूनी गुज़र कर हटाए जाएँ, व्यापारियों–किसानों के गले से ये फंदे ढीले किए जाएँ।

panels.shot: wide shot of toll barrier being dismantled as people watch
panels.action: At a former toll gate, guards and workers remove the wooden barrier and stack aside the posts; traders smile in disbelief, a young boy runs freely across, and Kulottunga rides past, offering a slight nod to the crowd.

dialogue.text: Barriers came down. Ferries that once demanded coin now bore grain and salt uncounted, while the news raced faster than the monsoon winds through village and bazaar.

i18n.hi: रुकावटें गिरा दी गईं। जो घाट पहले सिक्का माँगते थे, अब अनगिनत अनाज–नमक ढोते चले, और यह ख़बर गाँव–बाज़ार में मानसून से तेज़ फैल गई।

panels.shot: dynamic wide of a bustling port city with ships and warehouses
panels.action: A lively port scene shows large wooden ships anchored offshore, smaller craft unloading bales, warehouses open, and lines of merchants and laborers moving freely between quay and town without any toll house in sight.

dialogue.text: From Kaveripattinam to the Godavari ports, traffic thickened. Goods flowed more swiftly, markets swelled, and guild banners fluttered proudly in the seaward breeze.

i18n.hi: कावेरीपट्टनम से गोदावरी के बंदरगाह तक रास्ते भर गए। माल तेज़ी से बहा, बाज़ार फूले, और समुद्री हवा में संघों के झंडे शान से लहराए।

panels.shot: medium shot of village assembly around an accountant and a temple
panels.action: In a village, elders sit with an accountant before a temple, tallying on palm leaves as farmers and artisans stand nearby; in the distance, fields of paddy shimmer under the sun, hinting at a good season.

dialogue.text: Temples and village assemblies now received revenues that no longer leaked away at every gate. The king had given up a stream of coin, to let a river of trade grow wider.

i18n.hi: मंदिरों और ग्राम सभाओं को अब वह आय मिली जो हर फाटक पर टपक कर नहीं बह जाती थी। राजा ने सिक्कों की छोटी धारा छोड़ी, ताकि व्यापार की बड़ी नदी चौड़ी हो सके।

panels.shot: low-angle medium shot of Kulottunga at a temple, listening to a recitation
panels.action: Kulottunga stands near a temple mandapa, hands folded, as a temple reciter chants from an inscription, nearby villagers listening with quiet admiration; the king’s face shows restrained satisfaction.

dialogue.text: In stone and song, a new title clung to him. Inscriptions in sacred courtyards called him Sungam Tavirtta Cholan, the Chola who abolished the tolls.

i18n.hi: पत्थर और गीतों में उस पर नया बिरुद चिपक गया। पवित्र प्रांगणों के शिलालेख उसे ‘सुनगम तविरत्ता चोलन’, यानी चुंगी मिटाने वाला चोल पुकारते हैं।

panels.shot: close-up on Kulottunga’s thoughtful face at dusk on palace terrace
panels.action: At dusk on a palace terrace, Kulottunga leans on a parapet, looking over the silhouetted city and distant river, his face lined with years, eyes thoughtful yet steady.

dialogue.text: He still fought and bargained, for empire could not live on mercy alone. Yet he had chosen, once, to set aside an easy revenue in favor of a lighter yoke upon his realm.

i18n.hi: वह फिर भी लड़ा, सौदे भी किए — क्योंकि साम्राज्य सिर्फ़ दया पर नहीं टिकता। पर उसने एक बार आसान आमदनी को छोड़, अपने राज्य पर से जुए का भार हल्का करना चुना।

panels.shot: wide shot of a temple wall inscription with Kulottunga’s name highlighted by light
panels.action: Sunlight slants across a massive temple wall inscription, illuminating the carved line where Kulottunga’s titles, including Sungam Tavirtta, are meticulously etched, while a lone pilgrim walks by in the shadow below.

dialogue.text: Centuries would pass, dynasties fade, but stone kept the memory. Scholars would one day trace the characters that praised a king not just for conquest, but for remitting burdens.

i18n.hi: सदियाँ बीतीं, राजवंश ढले, पर पत्थर ने याद बचाए रखी। विद्वान उन अक्षरों को पढ़ेंगे जो एक ऐसे राजा की प्रशंसा करते हैं जिसने सिर्फ़ जीत नहीं, बोझ भी घटाए।

panels.shot: timeless montage wide of caravans, villagers, and a distant temple under open sky
panels.action: A timeless scene blends a caravan moving along a clear road, villagers exchanging goods in a shaded marketplace, and a temple tower rising calm under an open sky, all bound by invisible paths of trade.

dialogue.text: From his age, a lesson travels still: a ruler is remembered less for what he takes than for what he lifts away, for the unseen weight he removes from the shoulders of his people.

i18n.hi: उसके युग से एक सीख आज तक चलती है: शासक की पहचान ज़्यादा इस बात से होती है कि वह क्या छुड़ाता है, न कि क्या वसूलता है — वह अदृश्य भार जो वह अपने लोगों के कंधों से उतार देता है।

title_i18n.en: Kulottunga, the King Who Abolished Tolls
title_i18n.hi: कुलोतुंग, वह राजा जिसने चुंगी मिटा दी

subtitle_i18n.en: Sungam Tavirtta Cholan – The Emperor Who Loosened the Yoke
subtitle_i18n.hi: सुनगम तविरत्ता चोलन – जुए को हल्का करने वाला सम्राट
